advantage_and_disadvantage_math

Figure 1. Chance of raw d20 result based on rolling with advantage, normally, or with disadvantage.

Figure 2. Examples of damage distributions for two raw d20 rolls (5 & 15) by roll type (advantage/normal/disadvantage) and hit type (crit. hit/regular/crit. miss) for a character with a weapon that deals 1d8 damage (ability bonus excluded).

Figure 3. (A) Mean expected damage for a character with a weapon dealing 1d8 damage (ability bonus excluded) by raw d20 roll and roll type (advantage/normal/disadvantage). (B) Differences in expected mean damage for advantage and disadvantage rolls relative to normal rolls.

\(\overline{D}\): Mean expected damage

\(\overline{d}\): Mean die roll

\(r\): raw d20 roll

\(\overline{D}_{normal} = 1.1\overline{d} - 0.05\overline{d}r\)

\(\overline{D}_{advantage} = 1.095\overline{d} + 0.005\overline{d}r - 0.0025\overline{d}r^2\)

\(\overline{D}_{disadvantage} = 1.105\overline{d} - 0.105\overline{d}r + 0.0025\overline{d}r^2\)
